name: BTC 4h Retest Hold Timeout v1
edge_mechanism: a structural break is retested and held, but exposure is cut by time if expansion does not appear quickly, reducing slow-bleed continuation trades
thesis: The timeout-managed retest idea still deserves one cleaner test in cycle 84, but only if the trigger grammar is simple enough to plausibly trade. BTC 4h is the correct home lane because it is the primary lane, more transitional than ETH, and less late-continuation-biased than TAO. The edge is the held retest plus strict time discipline, not hoping management can rescue a weak entry.
invalidation_condition: If BTC 4h retest holds remain too sparse or time-based de-risking still cannot prevent bleed, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price breaks a local boundary, retests that level, holds it, and should expand quickly if the continuation is real.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Real continuation often reveals itself quickly after an accepted retest. If expansion does not appear fast, the trade is often stale or wrong, so timeout de-risking should cut that failure mode.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is the best fit because it is the live primary lane and should offer more retest density than ETH while still avoiding the late-entry bias of TAO trend continuation.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: BTC may still be too rotational, turning retest-hold setups into false positives even with timeout discipline.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but timeout is too aggressive, extend the de-risk clock slightly before loosening the retest filter.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A fair number of retest attempts, many early scratches, and only a minority of fast-expanding continuations that justify the structure.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 4h clears density and timeout de-risking materially reduces slow-bleed continuation losses.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or a profile where retest entries still bleed despite the timeout rule.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because retest-hold grammar is simple and recurring enough for 4h mixed regimes

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit primary lane for timeout-protected retest continuation.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h shows enough retest-hold events and timeout de-risking reduces bleed.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h remains too sparse or too rotational for the structure to matter.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with quick feedback on whether continuation is real.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same timeout-protected retest grammar survives on the flatter similar lane.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h preserves enough retest-hold continuation with cleaner structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h is too flat or too sparse for the mechanism.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Cleaner, slightly lower-count retest continuations than BTC.

validation_basket.why_included: Tests whether the same retest logic gains density on 1h without falling back into the intraday failure pattern.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 1h increases count while preserving actual retest-and-hold structure.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h reintroduces noisy churn and timeout dependency.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Higher count, smaller retest continuations than BTC 4h.

long: Time-based de-risk: cut 50% after 4 bars if no 0.80 ATR expansion
short: Time-based de-risk: cut 50% after 4 bars if no 0.80 ATR expansion
